scenario: Granite Marketplace exposes a webhook to 6000 events per day in Camunda 8.8. The team wants an inbound connector to receive HTTP POST callbacks at a configured URL, correlate each call to a waiting process instance by orderId, and complete the message subscription without writing custom HTTP listener code.
question: Which Camunda 8 inbound connector mechanism handles synchronous HTTP webhook callbacks?

options.text: The Webhook inbound connector exposes a URL endpoint; the runtime publishes a message correlated by the configured key expression on each HTTP POST
The Webhook inbound connector polls the configured URL every 30 seconds; the runtime publishes a message correlated by the configured key expression on changes
The Webhook inbound connector listens on a Kafka topic; the runtime publishes a message correlated by the configured key expression on each new record
The Webhook inbound connector subscribes to a JMS queue; the runtime publishes a message correlated by the configured key expression on each delivered message

a.text: Correct. The Camunda 8 Webhook inbound connector exposes an HTTP endpoint; on each POST it publishes a Zeebe message correlated by the configured key expression.

b.text: Incorrect. Webhook is event-driven HTTP, not polling; the polling style is a different inbound type (e.g., REST polling).

c.text: Incorrect. Kafka inbound is a separate connector; Webhook specifically denotes HTTP.

d.text: Incorrect. JMS is not the Webhook connector; queue-based subscriptions are different connector types.

explanation: The Webhook inbound connector exposes an HTTP endpoint; on each POST the runtime publishes a Zeebe message correlated by the configured key expression.
